meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering Project Management to Scale SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn how to implement professional project management workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your developer portfolio.
content.answer_block: To break through the intermediate plateau, shift from coding features to managing systems. By integrating Antigravity for architecture modeling and Claude Code for automated execution, you transform your development process into a scalable workflow. This professional approach moves your projects from simple scripts to production-ready SaaS applications.
content.intro:
You have built the to-do lists, the weather apps, and the basic CRUD interfaces. You know the syntax, but you feel the ceiling—that frustrating barrier where your code becomes a tangled web of technical debt the moment you add a second feature. The transition from 'coder' to 'engineer' isn't about learning a new language; it is about mastering the invisible architecture that holds a project together.

Today, we stop writing random functions and start architecting systems. By leveraging Antigravity for structural planning and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you will bridge the gap between student projects and professional-grade SaaS. It is time to treat your code with the rigor it deserves.

steps.title: Define the Domain Model
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your entities and relationships to ensure your database schema is robust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements for a project management SaaS and generate a relational schema including users, projects, tasks, and team permissions with clear foreign key constraints.

steps.title: Establish the API Contract
steps.description: Define your endpoints using OpenAPI standards to prevent interface drift during development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.0 specification for the project management backend, focusing on CRUD operations for tasks and state transitions.

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Ensure your frontend handles task states (e.g., To-Do, In-Progress, Done) consistently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine logic for a kanban board that handles optimistic updates and error states for task drag-and-drop actions.

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Write tests that mirror real-world user workflows to ensure your features don't break as you scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests for the task assignment flow, ensuring that permissions are validated and database state is updated correctly.

steps.title: Refactor for Performance
steps.description: Analyze your codebase for bottlenecks and optimize database queries using AI-driven insights.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for N+1 query patterns and suggest optimized database access patterns for the task-fetching endpoint.

pro_tips: Always use a 'Service Layer' pattern to decouple your business logic from your API routes.
Treat your README.md as a living document; if a feature isn't documented, it doesn't exist.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests before you write the actual feature code (TDD approach).
Keep your database migrations small and atomic to avoid deployment conflicts.
Monitor your API latency from day one; premature optimization is bad, but performance awareness is mandatory.
resources: React State Management Best Practices: https://react.dev/learn/managing-state
content.next_steps: Once you have completed this project structure, deploy your app to a staging environment and perform a load test to see how your architecture handles concurrent data mutations.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building 'toy' apps and start engineering professional software. Stop wasting time on spaghetti code and start building with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
